message:
fix(membrane): seed EWMAs on first observation and fix LZ76 self-extending match bound

Two correctness fixes in the runtime math module:

1. Birkhoff Ergodic Monitor EWMA initialization:
   Previously, the first observation was blended with the zero-initialized
   EWMA accumulators using the exponential weight (e.g., fast_mean starts
   at 0.0 and first update is `0 + alpha*(x - 0)`). This systematically
   underestimates the true mean during warmup, especially when early
   severity values are non-zero. Now, when count==1, both fast/slow EWMAs
   and the convergence gap trackers are seeded directly with the observed
   value, eliminating the cold-start bias.

2. LZ76 complexity function off-by-one:
   The inner match loop had a `j + m < i` bound preventing matches from
   extending past the dictionary/lookahead boundary. In the LZ76 algorithm
   (unlike LZ77), the current phrase can self-extend by referencing itself
   as it grows. Removing this bound allows the algorithm to correctly
   detect self-referential matches, producing accurate complexity estimates
   for repetitive sequences.

Changes:
- birkhoff_ergodic.rs: Add count==1 branch to seed EWMAs and gap trackers
  directly from first observation instead of blending with zero
- lempel_ziv.rs: Remove `j + m < i` guard from inner match loop to allow
  LZ76 self-extending matches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
